title: How to Get Payment Gateways for Cameroon Digital Products Live Before the End of the Day
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: Selling digital products from Cameroon? Traditional payment gateways like PayPal and Stripe aren’t options. Bank restrictions and KYC hurdles block creators like you. But you don’t need them. Blockchain payments remove these barriers. Today, you can set up a system that lets buyers in Douala, Yaoundé, or anywhere globally pay you directly in crypto. No middlemen. No delays. This guide shows you how to get your payment gateway live before the end of the day. Skip the red tape. Start collecting payments in BTC, ETH, USDT, and 300+ coins. Your digital products—ebooks, courses, art—can be sold globally. Today.
What You Can Actually Get Done Today

Today, you’ll set up a payment gateway that works globally. First, sign up with NOWPayments—they support 300+ coins and require no KYC. Next, integrate their API into your site or use their hosted payment page. Test a transaction with a small amount of crypto to ensure it works. Finally, add a payment button or QR code to your product pages. Buyers scan, pay, and get instant access. No coding required if you use their pre-built tools. By tonight, your store will accept payments from anywhere, including Cameroon. Focus on these steps. Skip everything else.
The Minimum Viable Setup That Works

You don’t need a full-fledged e-commerce site. Start with a simple landing page. Host it on GitHub Pages or any free platform. Add a payment button linked to NOWPayments. Include a clear description of your product and a download link. Automate delivery using a tool like Gumroad’s free tier or a basic email script. For crypto payments, use multi-chain QR codes. Buyers scan, pay, and get their product. This setup takes less than two hours. It’s barebones but functional. Perfect for your first sale.
Skipping the Steps That Do Not Matter Yet

Forget custom designs, analytics dashboards, or email marketing. Those come later. Right now, focus on getting paid. Use a simple payment button or QR code. Host your product on a free file-sharing site if needed. Skip KYC by using NOWPayments—they don’t require it. Ignore bank accounts or PayPal. Crypto payments bypass these entirely. Your goal today is one working transaction. Everything else is noise.
The blockchain has no map of restricted countries — a wallet in Lagos processes identically to one in London.
Getting Your First Transaction Live

Test your setup with a small transaction. Send crypto from your wallet to your NOWPayments address. Ensure the payment is confirmed and the product delivers automatically. If it works, share your payment link with one trusted buyer. Ask them to test it. Once they confirm, your system is live. Now, promote your product. Share it on social media, forums, or directly with your audience. Your first sale proves the system works. Scale from there.
What to Do in the Next 24 Hours

Tomorrow, refine your setup. Add a thank-you page with download instructions. Automate email receipts using Zapier or a simple script. If you’re selling multiple products, organize them on a single page. Test different payment methods—BTC, ETH, USDT—to see what buyers prefer. Finally, start promoting. Share your store link with your network. Your goal is to get 10 buyers in the next week. Keep it simple. Keep it moving.
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

Creators in Africa can sell digital products internationally by using blockchain-based payments. Platforms like NOWPayments let you accept crypto from anywhere, no KYC required. Buyers pay in BTC, ETH, or USDT, and you get paid directly to your wallet. This bypasses geographic restrictions and banking limitations. For example, if you’re in Nigeria, Ghana, or Kenya, buyers in the US or Europe can purchase your ebooks or courses instantly. The key is using a payment gateway that supports multiple cryptocurrencies and doesn’t require a bank account. This works in Cameroon, Pakistan, Bangladesh, and beyond.
Creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are already using this infrastructure to get paid globally.

Your Action Checklist

1. Sign up with NOWPayments to accept 300+ coins without KYC. 2. Add a payment button or QR code to your product page. 3. Test a small transaction to ensure it works. 4. Share your payment link with one trusted buyer. 5. Once confirmed, promote your product on social media or forums. 6. Add a thank-you page with download instructions. 7. Geographic neutrality is not a feature of crypto payments — it is the fundamental property of how blockchains work.
